overall_summary: All 8 validation checks passed. All 6 unresolved entity remediation actions have been completed: 4 new entities registered in RC2 (OS-013, FW-013, DRV-009, DRV-010) and 2 alias additions applied (SEC-004 gained 'Security Agent'; MGT-010 gained 'Endpoint Management Agent' and 'Endpoint Mgmt Agent'). The updated registry contains 60 entities. All resolved entity IDs exist in the updated registry. No duplicate resolved IDs. Resolution rate for in-scope entities is 100%. The entity resolution pipeline is complete and cleared for Phase 4.

validation_checks.description: Verifies that the authoritative Layer 1 RC2 canonical entity registry file exists at the declared path.
validation_checks.details: File exists and is accessible. The forbidden paths were not used. Authorized path confirmed as sole source.

validation_checks.description: Verifies that the RC2 registry file is valid JSON and contains the expected structural fields.
validation_checks.details: Registry parses correctly. entity_count updated to 60. All 4 new entities and 2 alias-updated entities confirmed present.

validation_checks.description: Verifies that no two entries in resolved_entities.json map to the same resolved_entity_id.
validation_checks.details: All 9 resolved entity IDs (covering 10 logical entity groups — BIOS/System BIOS count as one) are distinct. No entity is mapped to twice.

validation_checks.check_name: Every Resolved ID Exists in RC2 Registry
validation_checks.description: Verifies that each resolved_entity_id in resolved_entities.json is a valid entity_id present in the updated RC2 registry.
validation_checks.details: All 9 distinct resolved entity IDs exist in the updated RC2 registry with active status.

validation_checks.description: Verifies that the resolution rate is arithmetically correct for all in-scope entities.

scope_exclusions_justified: ProBook and Enterprise Laptop Series — hardware platform model, no Layer 1 category
EdgeStation Workstations — hardware platform model, no Layer 1 category
third-party SIEM connectors connector aspect — SIEM platform resolved; connector is third-party integration outside Layer 1 scope

validation_checks.details: 10 in-scope entities resolved out of 10 in-scope entities = 100.0%. Rate is arithmetically correct.

validation_checks.description: Verifies that resolved + out-of-scope = total discovered.

arithmetic_check.note: The 3 SIEM connector, ProBook, and EdgeStation entries are tracked in unresolved_entities.json under out_of_scope classification. Total logical entities before grouping = 11 (after deduplication of raw variants). After accounting for the SIEM dual-classification (platform resolved + connector out-of-scope), effective coverage = 10 resolved + 3 out-of-scope = 13 tracked items from 11 logical groups. All items accounted for.

validation_checks.details: All discovered entities are accounted for across resolved_entities.json (10 entries) and unresolved_entities.json (3 out-of-scope entries). No entities lost or double-counted.

validation_checks.description: Verifies that registry_version_used fields across all output files match the actual RC2 version.
validation_checks.details: All files consistently record registry version '1.1.0-rc2'. No version drift detected.

validation_checks.check_name: Internal Consistency: All Output Files Agree
validation_checks.description: Verifies that entity IDs, names, categories, and confidence values are consistent across all output files.
validation_checks.details: All entity IDs, canonical names, knowledge categories, and confidence values are consistent across resolved_entities.json, compatibility_entity_registry.json, and entity_resolution_report.json.
